ai_disclosure: Grounded in real public data (weather via Open-Meteo, public holidays via Nager.Date) where available, phrased by AI (Gemini) and AI copy-reviewed against the saudade editorial rules. Written for this edition's readers — not translated from English. No human rewrite.

items.headline: The Changdeokgung courtyard stays quiet under a 31°C afternoon sky.
items.lede: Temperatures hover between 24 and 31°C today as thin clouds drift across the capital.
items.body: Temperatures hover between 24 and 31°C today as thin clouds drift across the capital. Afternoon humidity remains noticeable, though fine particulate readings stay comfortably low at PM2.5 11. Travelers exchanging currency will find rates holding near 1 USD to 1473 KRW in most downtown banks. It is a suitable afternoon for indoor contemplation, particularly as the 'Choe-Dan' exhibition at Seoul continues to showcase traditional Korean decorative art until July 29. Afterward, quiet observers might walk toward the historic alleys to notice how local establishments continue to evolve, such as the newly expanded Bagel Seoul bringing fresh options to the central district after 3 years of success. Keep a light layer handy for air-conditioned galleries.

items.headline: Evening strolls along the Han River catch the fading light at 19:47.
items.lede: Sunset arrives at 19:47, leaving a soft, partly cloudy haze over the water.
items.body: Sunset arrives at 19:47, leaving a soft, partly cloudy haze over the water. The daytime high reaches 31°C before cooling toward the 24°C evening floor. The air remains fair with a stable PM2.5 reading of 11, making riverbanks pleasant for walking after dark. Financial transactions settle around 1 USD to 1473 KRW. Visitors interested in cultural crosscurrents can still catch performances or exhibitions downtown, including the recent Yale a cappella group bringing K-pop harmonies to Seoul gallery spaces. Quiet walkers will appreciate the slower pace in residential lanes away from major transit hubs. The evening air carries the faint scent of damp earth from recent summer moisture.

items.headline: Traditional craft galleries offer cool respite from the midday warmth.
items.lede: The morning starts at 24°C and climbs steadily under partly cloudy skies.
items.body: The morning starts at 24°C and climbs steadily under partly cloudy skies toward 31°C. Exchange kiosks operate at 1 USD to 1473 KRW, and city air quality is recorded at a fair PM2.5 level of 11. Travelers planning museum itineraries can include the 'Choe-Dan' exhibition before it closes on July 29, offering a deep look into traditional Korean decorative art. Meanwhile, the local culinary scene sees quiet expansion, evidenced by the popular Boulangerie Seoul opening Bagel Seoul downtown following 3 years of success. Public transit runs with standard punctuality across all lines. Carry a reusable water bottle and take frequent breaks in shaded courtyard gardens.

items.headline: A brief 0.2mm drizzle touches the sidewalks of Shiba Park.
items.lede: Light rain leaves streets damp as temperatures range from 24 to 32°C.
items.body: Light rain leaves streets damp as temperatures range from 24 to 32°C. The precipitation total stays minimal at 0.2mm, but the UV index climbs aggressively to 8, classified as very high. Travelers should note that air quality registers as poor with PM2.5 at 18. Currency exchanges trade at approximately 1 USD to 163 JPY. For travelers seeking shelter, nearby cultural institutions offer quiet spaces, including the Library Hotel in Tokyo where visitors find meaningful encounters through books at Shiba Park Hotel. The Edo-Tokyo Museum exhibition tracing the roots of Western architecture in Japan also provides a thoughtful afternoon escape from the humid streets. Wear sun protection despite the overcast drizzle.

items.body: Daytime warmth peaks at 32°C before descending to a humid 24°C night. A trace of precipitation measured at 0.2mm has mostly evaporated from the asphalt. Film enthusiasts might also look into the short films featured at the Tokyo Horror Film Festival 2026. Keep windows closed if sensitive to particulate matter.

items.body: Temperatures peak at 32°C after a morning low of 24°C. Only 0.2mm of light drizzle falls, leaving the air heavy and humid. items.headline: Partly cloudy skies temper the 26°C afternoon heat along the Tagus.

items.body: Temperatures range from 20 to 26°C with a very high UV index of 8. The air quality remains fair and clean, registering a low PM2.5 of 8. items.body: The morning begins fresh at 20°C before reaching a comfortable 26°C maximum. Partly cloudy skies offer intermittent shade from the UV index of 8.
